target.name: Rain Industries
target.founder: Bryan Hatton, Ephraim Nowak, Maxwell Brodie

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rain Industries is a Seed-stage drone and autonomous systems company founded by Bryan Hatton, Ephraim Nowak, and Maxwell Brodie that has raised $14.7M to date. The broader Drones & Autonomous sector includes five material private peers (Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air) and no public comparables, though a wider peer set of eight private companies exists in the sector.

The valuation analysis relies on Stage-Progression methodology, which is applicable given Rain's Seed stage. Using the $14.7M raised as a proxy for post-money valuation and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-Series A progression yields $37M. Peer-Median triangulation is not applicable; while eight Drones & Autonomous peers exist, none qualify as same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with sufficient data concentration to support reliable benchmarking. A broader sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the heterogeneity of company models and capital intensity across the peer set.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $31M to $42M, centered on $37M. This reflects conservative (–15%) and optimistic (+15%) variance around the Stage-Progression result. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow-set peer anchors, limited visibility into Rain's specific product maturity and go-to-market traction, and sector-wide variability in capital efficiency and time-to-commercialization. An updated range would benefit from additional same-stage or recent Series A funding data within the autonomous systems subsector.
